The Creation Myth (1:1-24):
... e na alvorada dos dias estava Wyrhta, o poder que cria.
Onde Nada reinava supremo, Wyrhta deu vida a Tudo;
moldou aqui e ali, gerou o agora e o antes,
formou o um e, em coro, o outro.
E onde a atração surgiu,
a natureza selvagem prosperou e multiplicou ...

... mas das partes indomadas, Wielda apareceu, o poder que governa:
o poder da violência deliberada, que dobra aqui e ali,
que se curva agora e antes de acordo com ideais e anseios;
que colhe um e rejeita outro.
Com o tempo, o apetite de Wielda cresceu,
e o nascimento era inevitável ...

... porque violência gera ódio, que gera Wratha, o poder que reage.
Nascida da fome de Wielda pelo projeto de Wyrhta, Wratha está em todos os lugares e em lugar nenhum,
sempre perto de onde mãe encontra mãe,
sempre crescendo em poder com a força da violência.
A chegada do fruto negro está profetizada,
tão inescapável quanto terrível ...

... e acontece, aconteceu e acontecerá novamente,
que Wratha cresce sem limites, furiosa, cega em sua fome.
Acontece que a obscuridade ganha corpo e espírito e as mais afiadas das garras,
que o fruto espalha sementes que envenenam o primeiro,
na busca para suprimir o segundo.
Então Tudo morre...
The Fall of Symbar (2:1-60) ]:
... and king upon king
sent his people to die
in wave upon wave
over rolling plains;
men and women on
whose broken hulls,
an imposing realm
was built. Such was
the foundation of
Symbaroum: built
with flesh and blood.

... and the beings
from beyond tore
Symbaroum apart,
with claws and
fangs, horns and
spikes; invited but
not welcome, called
but not wanted; they
were the guests who
in insatiable hunger
ate and drank til
there was nothing
left to devour ...

...and the day
dawned, when the
spawn of the Serpent
took to arms, when
the crimes of countless
days must be counted
and atoned for, horn
by horn, fang by fang.
and the sinners wept
with blinded eyes, they
moaned with severed
throats, they fled on
fractured limbs. And
Symbaroum fel , into
dreamless sleep ...

By the end, at the dawn of the future,
blightlings were swarming, humans would run or be dead.
In the abyss, in darkness nocturnal: parents and spawn,
in combat 'til dawn, a slaughter to finish them all.
But the ending would hatch a beginning,
here we bravely create, a world that again,
will fall into ruin and silence.

I feel like a leaf through the eons,
from the branch of creation towards the here and now.
Below was a desolate mesa,
cut from the crust, from bedrock and dust,
the home of a ravenous god.
Like me he succumbed to the autumn,
sank beaten to rest by the heart of the stone.

South fled the trodden and vanquished,
with darkness behind, with warmness and sunlight ahead.
A fortress they raised on the lowlands, a wonder of stones,
on the Guardian's bones, storing the relics of Symbar.
But blighted and torn were their bodies.
The sins of the fathers echoed in flesh,
and darkness devoured them all.
The Spider King Cycle (3:1-24):
... and there, in the
chittering, hissing
enemy swarm, there
fought Angathal's
spawn, erect and
proud. With spear
and axe, with drip-
ping jaws, arak-an
and arak-zanz
attacked the strong-
hold of Serembar ...

... and he who was
called 'first among
equals' was neverthe-
less the fastest, the
toughest and the most
skilled with the spear;
he was Maiesticar,
the pillager who
thrust Spiderbane
at the heart of the
Spider King, and all
the way through.
The Tragedies Cycle (4:1-120):
... and as Eregan
foretold: 'the wise
man's road is long,
twice as long through
murky woods; the
foolish man's road
is short, half as long
through murky woods,
cut short by fol y and
bloody bashings.

... and the hatred grew
between brother and
sister, Manaud the
Gluttonous and Manauad
the Wrathful, sent
monster against mosnter-
in raging battle. For
two moons, the people
suffered; bloodied,
eaten, crushed. Then
the battle was over; the
wounded victor a god
of the people, always
fearing the hunger
of the beaten ...

... and Kasion and
Bruto aimed their
blades, at noble
tyrant, towards
silverblack heart. In
the dusk of the throne
room were rebels
and prince, but also
a lurking danger;
the blood of the
rivals slowly merged
where broken bodies
soon fell down ...

... and the prince
of the west marched
bravely on, singing
horror, chanting
fear, to meet mighty
Uron in battle; the
serpent, the greatest,
consumed by dread,
struck the world in
trembling fear. For
nine full days, the terror
was all, 'til Uron
retreated in shame.

... and every serpent
heard the call ; in
line by line, row by
row, they covered the
slope by the gaping
cave; strongest
atop, weakest in the
shadows of the bale.
Fofar the Colossal,
the Destroyer, Lord
of Dragons, let the
fires wash over the
bowing crowd, as
far as it reached.
Then, no growls
were ever heard ...

... and Gorameios
filled his lair with
gold and jewels, stolen
from the dark vaults
of Saar-Atman; so
infected by taint were
the treasures, that
the serpent was suf-
focated into an early
death and the spirit
of the conjuror won
back the riches. In
the nest of Gorameios
does Saar-Atman
forever brood.

... and troll-mother
Vouax's cauldron,
lined with the words
of the weaver, smeared
with the fat of the
wardens, fel down
the abyss, fel into
oblivion. The vessel
brewing the downfall
of all may never
again be heated, not
by fire, not by the
dark of the Abyss.

... and tormented
the Howler roars in
agony; he who instead
of an eternal life was
awarded an eternally
reoccurring death;
he is Inja, the one
whose pain echoes
through Davokar each
time the juices of life
turn to vapors.

... and while the
body and power
were hers, the eyes
and desire were not;
words and deeds,
solace and lust, were
that of a hungering
spirit. But blind to
his dead and beloved,
the liege was unable
to see, the alien gaze,
the sundering claws,
that soon brought
his life to an end ...
The Iron Pact Cycle (5:1-92):
... and Cidriana
battered Creation,
with coarse words,
with forceful
gestures; wrenched
her will , and the
world with it. The
grounds raged, oozed
blackness, quivered
in pain, echoed
the violence and
torment; Wratha
had come to avenge
her beloved.

... and deep down the
world was violated,
because the shell is
suited for the force
within; to add power
to power without
bracing the hide is
as inexcusable as
it is inconsiderate.
For when the shell
ruptures the force
released is multiplied
by the thousands, from
anger and shame.

... and the malaise
lingers, in earth and
rock, in water and
air; besets all which
breathes, which
wanders and crawls
and flies. The gift of
Eneano must not be
shunned, nor be seen
as the salvation.

... and remember
from which roots
this verdant beauty
springs; even the
richest crop feeds
on decay, and never
before has any harvest
been as lush, or any
soil as black, as the
one in the shadowy
halls of Davokar.

... and under moss
and roots, under the
trails of crawlers
and wrigglers, there
are caverns basking
in the gloom of the
underworld; there
thrive implings and
the bone pale; there the
blight grows on rocky
walls and abominable
shel s; there the
weepers walk in the
Halls of a Thousand
Tears, round and
round a well about
to overflow...

... and the soul of
the seed was already
lost; paternal sins,
maternal atrocities
tainted the blood of
the offspring. To be
affected by darkness
while wandering
life is nothing like
carrying it in the
flesh; that is evil.

... and the search
goes on forever, for the
hidden Ambal Seba, where the truth is
spelled in gold, where
the keys to the primal
power are etched in
Circles, round and
round the thirteen
Pillars. See, the mists
will never fade.
Not even Fofar the
Destroyer can dispell
the veil over a valley
that does not exist.
